title: City Council Meeting Summary Agent
problem: Answering constituent and press questions spanning many past meetings requires searching scattered recordings and transcripts.
solution: Agent ingests meeting recordings and transcripts, produces structured summaries (agenda items, decisions, dissents, quotes), and exposes a Q&A interface for the full meeting history.
workflowSteps: Meeting recording and transcript uploaded after each meeting
Transcription agent produces text; summary agent extracts agenda items, decisions, dissents, notable quotes
Structured summaries indexed into searchable knowledge base
Q&A interface allows natural language queries across full meeting history
Responses cite specific meeting, date, and speaker
keyInsights: Longitudinal institutional memory is the value proposition. The key design decision is whether to store structured summaries or raw transcripts in the vector index — structured summaries with metadata filters are more cost-efficient and produce higher-precision retrieval.
mermaidDiagram:
flowchart TD
  A([Meeting Recording + Transcript]) --> B[Transcription Agent]
  B --> C[Summary Agent: Agenda + Decisions + Quotes]
  C --> D[Index with Meeting Metadata]
  D --> E([Natural Language Q&A Across Full Meeting History])
architectureNotes: Meeting audio or transcript is segmented by speaker-turn. Each segment is summarised and tagged by topic (budget, zoning, public comment, etc.). A synthesis agent merges per-topic summaries into a structured minutes document with action items and vote records. The output is posted to a public portal and emailed to subscribers automatically.
